message: So I finally got around to testing our smoke alarms last week—you know, the ones I've been saying I'd check "someday" for like two years. My partner gives me that look. The *look*. "Didn't you literally tell me we should do this every month?" I did. I absolutely did. And there I was, proving my own point in real time. Two of the three needed new batteries. Two! The smoke alarms I thought were protecting us were basically decorative at that point. I'm not gonna lie—getting called out by your own prior self is humbling. But also? It's kind of a gift. Because now they actually work, and I'm not lying awake wondering. Simple stuff like this—making sure your escape route is clear, keeping the kitchen clear of clutter, knowing where your nearest exit is—doesn't have to be complicated or stressful. It's just... doing the thing. Have you tested your alarms lately, or are you in the "someday" club with me?
